channel.title: Society on Fengyu WANG
channel.description: Recent content in Society on Fengyu WANG

item.title: Businesses with Long-Term High Margins
item.description:
Businesses that sustain high margins over the long term all revolve around a few deep and persistent drivers of human nature.

The first driver is greed. In ancient times it was gambling houses, in modern times it is finance. Stocks, crypto, futures, derivatives. No matter how sophisticated the financial instrument, the underlying psychological mechanism is the same: people believe they are smarter than others, that they will profit where others lose. Institutions do not make money from market movements. They make it from greed and overconfidence.

item.title: AI Development Creates Economic Urgency
item.description:
The rapid development of AI inevitably creates economic urgency. Its direct source is the mismatch between the speed of technological substitution and the speed of social adaptation.

Technology iterates on a monthly basis. Education systems, vocational training, and social safety nets adjust on an annual basis at best. When a persons skills go from scarce to redundant within six months, but vocational training cannot keep up, the urgency is real.

item.title: Guinea-Bissau: A Case Study in State Fragility
item.description:
Guinea-Bissau is an extreme example of governance failure. Since independence in 1974, the country has experienced multiple coups and periods of political turmoil, leaving state capacity extremely weak. This fragility is not accidental. It is the result of several structural factors working together.

The first is thesingularly dependent nature of the economy. Cashew nuts account for over ninety percent of export revenue. This extreme dependence makes the country highly vulnerable to international price fluctuations. The second is the fragmented nature of society. There are multiple ethnic groups and factions, and political loyalty is often based on ethnicity and family rather than national institutions. The third is the weakness of institutions. State-building has never been completed since independence. Formal institutions are hollowed out by informal relationships.

item.title: A Bank Teller Existential Crisis
item.description:
The first symptom of an existential crisis is a disturbance in the perception of time. When someone has sat at the same window for six years, time is no longer a linear river. It becomes an unmarked expanse of water.

At ten to nine in the morning, he puts on his uniform, fastens the buttons, and glances at the mirror in the changing room. The person in the mirror wears a navy blue suit with a badge on the lapel, his name and an employee number. He knows that in ten minutes, that number will matter more than his name. He walks to the counter, sits down, turns on the computer, enters his password. The system boots up. At nine oclock, the shutter rises. This is his sixth year at this bank, at this window.

item.title: The Rapid Development of Artificial Intelligence
item.description:
The rapid advancement of AI has a highly differentiated impact on different economies. For economies that occupy the lower-end of the technology chain and rely on large-scale labour as their engine, this impact first manifests as economic urgency.

Take parts of Southeast Asia as an example. Their past competitiveness did not come from technological advantage. It came from the scale and cost advantage of their labour force. This is a development path that compensates for efficiency through quantity. As AI increasingly replaces standardised intellectual and manual labour, the sustainability of this path is being fundamentally shaken.

item.title: Supply Is Strong, Demand Is Weak
item.description:
The core contradiction in the current economy is structural: supply exceeds effective demand. This is not absolute overcapacity across all industries, but a differentiated, squeezing imbalance.

Starting with consumer goods: manufacturing capacity across many sectors is ample. Daily necessities, home appliances, clothing, and ordinary industrial goods are in abundant supply, with prices trending downward and competition intensifying. But the problem is not on the supply side. It is that effective purchasing power cannot keep up. It is not that people do not need these goods. It is that their disposable income is shrinking and their consumption behaviour is contracting.

item.title: Zero-Sum Games, Arms Races, and Prisoner Dilemmas
item.description:
What people commonly call involution (neijuan) has several precise theoretical models in academic discourse. They explain the same phenomenon from different angles: everyone works harder, yet no one improves their position.

The most direct theory is the arms race. Two countries compete to build more weapons. One builds one hundred missiles, the other builds two hundred. The first builds three hundred more. In the end, both have exhausted their resources, but the balance of power has not changed. Apply this model to work and study: everyone works longer hours, pursues higher degrees, and collects more certificates, but relative rankings stay the same while absolute returns diminish.

item.title: Social Experiment Reality Shows
item.description:
Locking real people in an enclosed space, throwing them a crisis scenario, and letting them solve problems using their genuine personalities, knowledge, and experience. This format has existed in television and experimental theatre for years, with severalmature models.

The most precise classification is social experiment reality show plus immersive group survival game. It combines three elements: real social identities, an enclosed space, and a crisis challenge. The participants real identities as academics, scientists, businesspeople, artists, etc. determine their behaviour under crisis, which is completely different from scripted performances.

item.title: The DM-Only Phenomenon on Xiaohongshu
item.description:
Posts asking for recommendations on Xiaohongshu almost universally get replies saying DM me. On the surface, this looks like information opacity, but underneath it is a complete business logic and platform rule system.

From a business perspective, the essence of this behaviour is the commercialisation of information asymmetry. Giving an answer publicly means the value of that information instantly drops to zero. Keeping the answer behind a DM means the information can be priced, traded, and screened. Everyone who replies DM me is operating their own information asset.

item.title: Rent Extraction vs. Rent Creation in Regulation Theory
item.description:
Two types of intermediaries play fundamentally different roles in economic activity. One type creates value. The other distributes value. The criterion for distinguishing between the two defines how we understand the true source of value in markets.

Value-creating intermediaries, with Apple as the classic case, are characterised by thenovel integration of elements that could not previously be combined, creating products that did not previously exist. In Apple case, scattered elements such as ARM CPUs, touch technology, the iOS ecosystem, and the App Store were integrated into the iPhone, a product category that did not exist before. The definingcharacteristic of this type of entity is: without it, the corresponding new product would not exist. It has irreplaceable integration value.

item.title: Rock n Roll Is More Important Than Cannons
item.description:
If we use two sounds to metaphor the logic of social operation, cannons represent hard commands and rules, while rock n roll represents the impulse for expression and creation.

In a world of only cannons, all rules are fixed and unquestionable. People do not need to think; they only need to obey. Efficiency may be high, but the room for innovation is zero. In any system with only top-down commands and no bottom-up feedback, the system will eventually ossify.
